framework.name: Y=R+S+N Mathematical Decomposition Framework
framework.description: A mathematical framework for analyzing and decomposing content quality in research papers and technical documents

R_relevant.name: Relevant Content
R_relevant.description: Core systematic content that directly addresses the research objectives

excellent.description: Excellent paper, use as primary source

S_superfluous.name: Superfluous Content
S_superfluous.description: Marginally systematic content that provides context but is not essential

focused.description: Focused paper, gets to the point

unfocused.description: Lots of background, may be unfocused

N_noise.name: Noise Content
N_noise.description: True noise, errors, and irrelevant content that reduces paper quality

context_collapse.description: When bad or confusing information overwhelms good information

high.description: Dangerous, avoid or use extreme caution

types.poisoning: Absorption of misinformation and biased content
types.distraction: Information overload leading to focus loss
types.confusion: Integration of non-relevant or contradictory data

students: Use papers with Y Score > 0.8 as primary sources
Papers with Y Score 0.6-0.8 for supporting information
Avoid papers with Y Score < 0.5 unless necessary
Check Context Collapse Risk before citing
researchers: Aim for R > 70% and N < 15% in own papers
Prefer sources with low context collapse risk
Use framework to screen large numbers of papers
Document Y scores in literature review process
business_professionals: Evaluate technical reports using R scores
Be suspicious of high N scores (errors/unclear parts)
Assess quality of data and recommendations
Request Y=R+S+N analysis of vendor documentation

high_quality_paper.title: Mathematical Decomposition of Signal and Noise in Context Engineering Systems
high_quality_paper.content_sample: We present a robust mathematical framework for signal decomposition with comprehensive validation across multiple datasets
high_quality_paper.interpretation: Excellent paper, suitable for primary reference

survey_paper.title: Survey of Background Literature in Information Theory Applications
survey_paper.content_sample: This comprehensive survey provides extensive background information and literature review
survey_paper.interpretation: Good for background reading, not primary source

problematic_paper.title: Multidimensional Stochastic Analysis with Heterogeneous Nonlinear Components
problematic_paper.content_sample: Complex analysis with unclear mathematical formulations and ambiguous frameworks. Some equations contain formatting errors
problematic_paper.interpretation: Use with caution, validate key claims
